cards.back: An iterative and adaptive project management approach that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and rapid delivery
The ability of an organization or project to adjust to changing circumstances, requirements, or market conditions
The phenomenon where frequent changes or iterations lead to decreased productivity, morale, and overall performance
The additional expenses or resource requirements associated with adapting to changes, such as rework, overhead, and lost productivity
A metaphor describing how frequent or excessive changes can lead to a bucket that eventually overflows, causing inefficiencies and decreased quality
